introduction: As Elexion Automotive continues to innovate in the electric vehicle industry, the need to safeguard our vehicles and customers from potential cyber threats is more crucial than ever. This document outlines the cybersecurity measures we have implemented, how they contribute to the overall security posture of our organization, and potential improvements going forward. The purpose of this report is to provide an overview of our cybersecurity practices and ensure our key stakeholders are well informed.

subsections.heading: Historical Overview of Cybersecurity at Elexion
subsections.content: Elexion Automotive has always been committed to cybersecurity since its inception. In 2011, the company invested in a dedicated cybersecurity department to ensure the safety of its systems. By 2012, the department grew by 25% to handle the increasing complexity of our electric vehicles' software.
subsections.justification: This content provides historical data and growth of cybersecurity department but not their measures.

subsections.heading: Current Cybersecurity Strategies and Implementations
subsections.content: As of 2020, Elexion Automotive's cybersecurity team consists of over 300 personnel, representing 16% of our total workforce. Our team is responsible for securing all digital facets of our operations, from manufacturing to customer service. They regularly collaborate with the software development team to ensure the safety of our platforms.
subsections.justification: The information discusses team size and collaboration, not specific cybersecurity measures.

subsections.heading: Future Directions for Enhancing Cybersecurity Measures
subsections.content: Moving forward, Elexion Automotive is dedicated to staying at the forefront of cybersecurity measures in the automotive sector. We are planning to increase our cybersecurity department's budget by 20% in the next fiscal year. This will facilitate the procurement of advanced tools and recruitment of additional experts.
subsections.justification: Content discusses future plans for the department's growth but not specific cybersecurity measures.

conclusion: The safety and security of our customers and their data is paramount to Elexion Automotive. Our ongoing commitment to cybersecurity reflects this, and we are continually exploring new measures and practices to stay ahead of potential threats. We will persist in our efforts to strengthen our cybersecurity framework and will share updates and progress reports regularly. We also welcome any feedback or suggestions to enhance our cybersecurity practices.
